column1: Our system now has a track... the NHC has designated the tropical wave as PTC 4. The track shows a tropical storm somewhere along our coast by Sunday morning. Tropical Storm Warnings and Watches are now in effect. I'll post that map next.
From what I'm seeing, it looks like the worst will be in the morning, daylight hours? Much better than an overnight storm! I never sleep on those occasions.
same! I hate the at night storms, I never sleep either!
I sleep like a teenager during hurricanes. 😆
I so agree. I always hate the 2a storms... Somehow scary during those hours... 😕
especially if the power goes out, then u hear strange noises, I can't handle that 😞
Agree. I have those little lights that Denis Phillips touted right before Irma, but sometimes those freaky little lights can make it even scarier.... 🙄
if our power goes out (we live in Orlando), the noises anyone hears is my screaming, “oh, heavens, NO!”
I love it! Time to get my boogie board out! 😁
when I was a kid we lived near a big ditch and after a storm we'd take our boogie boards out an play in the ditch 😂
careful what you ask for
I was out in the last storm we had in April at Bonita Beach in 4-5 waves it was awesome! 😎 I was the only one on the beach 😆
AMEN 🙏
not a good idea at all !
Doesn’t sound like it will be any worse for us than the storm we got the other night that knocked our power out for 2 hours.
but this involves almost the whole state
might need to test out the new generator we got during the tax holiday!
FYI, just finished cutting my lawn for the second time this week 😂😂
I just cut mine today 🤣
us too
I know this is just a tropical storm this time however, if this is the future for this season this track is very scary!!!
Years ago we had a TS that devastated this area around Tampa!!
I remember that one well.
which one? I’m trying to remember but it’s all a blur?
which one? I’ve lived here for 42 years and am drawing a blank on a time when Tampa was “devastated”
been here almost 50 yrs devasted is a strong word but some of the tropical waves/depressions/storms have caused issues with the bay area worst I can remember is the no name storm many years ago

Tropical Storm Debby in 2012. I am on the beach we had massive flooding, beach erosion, water was almost to my knees, streets flooded for days could not leave, tornados. The entire island was under water. I think we got 10-15” of rain.
exactly- it do remember- it was tropical storm Debby?!?! I’ve been thinking the same thing !
oh I remember the no name storm aka “ the storm of the century”! I’ll never forget how the wind how that night and then the water came up and flooded my grandparents house in South Tampa!
I agree! Love when they track north in the Atlantic and remain as fish storm.

I guess I'll be getting my dogs up nice and early for their morning duty.
Hope us dog loves have time and it doesn't linger to long.
if you have a covered porch, get some puppy pads and they can go on those at least.

my husband, and I actually purchase a couple of pieces of sod and cut open a big
garbage bag to lay it on and lay pads out as well so that they have a choice.
You and me both. 😂. Mine hear it outside and won't even look out a window. 😂
Awwww. Mine tries to get under the bed he just shivers non stop. The other one just barks non stop. So I try and keep them in bed and watch a loud movie if possible. Great Danes here ❤️
great Pyrenees here 🐩. Poor doggies.

I give two of
my four Composure and they wear thunder vests.
The other two could give a hoot .
my Sister has two dane's and a doberman.
If I would let my husband that's the combination of what we would have. He loves the Danes but wants a doberman too 😂. Our female wouldn't have it though she is a bossy boss 😂😂.
I just said the same thing to my husband
what about Delray Beach

Looks underwhelming at this point. Thanks for keeping us informed, as always, Denis! Will never forget being so panicked my first hurricane season here and someone told me to follow you — now I never worry until Rule No. 7!
exactly. You can tell who’s new to Florida by the comments.
I had had six weather apps no more. Denis 👍 is the way to go.
Thinking about all the people who are just moving back on Fort Myers Beach and Sanibel.
It sorta looks like most of the afternoon storms we have been getting lately, though, I think it will just be rain and a little wind 🙂
I'm not going to Disney and I don't have any flights scheduled, but I am very interested in what you think the surge will be and when.

Our property is up high enough. My EV may just need to stay in the garage… 🤣 The entrance to our property has flooded in the past. I don’t want to see what happens if I submerge the bottom of the EV in water. Thank goodness for two ICE trucks.

we live in Tampa bay on the water and received the following alert, but is subject to change: STORM SURGE - LATEST LOCAL FORECAST: Localized storm surge possible - Peak Storm Surge Inundation: The potential for 1-3 feet above ground somewhere within surge prone areas - Window of concern: Begins Saturday evening
it it going to hit Delray Beach
that’s the east coast of Florida
meaning yes or no I’m sorry
same. We need surge info
It’ll change at last minute like always watch 😂
Are there any highs, lows or even the jet stream driving this system at all, or is it just drifting on it's own??
it’s dealing with a trough which is supposed to grab it and a high pressure which is supposed to let it through
Ah, the good ole roulette wheel... 🙂
tons and tons of that info in his previous posts
So, basically. That "ship" spaghetti track which was the ONLY one showing it going RIGHT thru us in Largo.. was right.
The one they keep saying is always “wrong”. Funny how that works.
yep, lol. So should we pay more attention to the Ship than the Euro from here on out?
This is literally like an afternoon storm if these numbers hold true.
I hope it waits till my flight home Sunday
Like most folks, I've been keeping up with tropical systems most of my adult life, but I admit, I've never heard the media refer to it this way, i.e., "Invest 97L." Am I the only one?
it's always an invest before it meets the criteria for a named storm. They start "investigating" when the conditions are favorable for development.
I get that. Just never heard media use that term.
interesting. I've heard it used frequently since we moved here in 2021.
to be fair. We cut the cord years ago and rely on X and direct news links. 🙂
me either and I’ve lived in Florida for 50 years, 25 of them in the panhandle with lots of hurricanes. Never heard the term.
it’s always been like this it’s an invest before a cyclone or name storm
I've just never heard it reported that way.
it’s been like that in the 27 years of my existent
never heard it either and consider myself a bit of a news hound.
thanks! Same.
Oh surprise.... every storm track is straight through pinellas. I feel like the people who create these are people who play roulette and keep betting black because "it's gotta come up eventually "
well this isn’t through Pinellas. It’s off the coast.
what map are you looking at?
the same one you just circled that has it off our coast. Yes we’ll get rain but rule #7

Thank you Denis , been here 35 years and so many new peeps here in Florida . Well Guys you all chose to move here and this is one of the things every summer, Get used to it and stop panicking . Just have a plan
this is who you need to follow for the best local info. Do NOT watch the weather channel lol they are all about hyping it up!
Interesting I have gotten alert for Miami Dade, Broward, Palm Beach and going up the coast collier and Fort Myers! At the end of Broward beginning of Palm Beach I guess we’ll wait and see
My mom is flying out of Miami Sunday morning, will her flight be delayed?

Are we sure with this one now, as this storm been like a pin ball. Up the East Coast through the Caribbean to the East Coast of the Gulf then up through the panhandle and now it’s making land in pinellas 😂
where do you see landfall in Pinellas?
On the post earlier from Dennis
it was between pinellas and Levy county so basically Hillsbourgh and Pasco
lol
Can y'all stop freaking out?! Tampa gets 50 mph winds every afternoon! 🤣🤣🤣🤣
Thank you for keeping us on the realistic end of our Florida weather. Plan, prepare, be smart and remember Rule #7
Sooo...when a storm makes landfall North of TB, it pulls water out of the bay...correct????
no opposite

The storms rotate counter clockwise.
South of the bay, pulls water out of the bay.
North of the bay pushes water into the bay.
Be aware of low lying coastal areas as the water levels could drop with the storm approaching from the south, then rapidly rise as the storm moves north. Escape could get cut off by rapidly rising storm surge.
Tocobaga Tribe is making sure it doesn’t become a big hurricane!
absolutely!!!
st. Pete and Tampa bay are always protected
you ain’t wrong!
Hoping our Sunday cruise is good to go!

Do you think it has potential to turn into a hurricane?
Should I put my shutters on my house?
Pool needs the water. Outstanding!
dang I've had to let few inches out of mine several times in the last few weeks! Tons of rain in Crystal River
me too! I felt like I was draining some water every few days for the last month.
me too! Tons of rain here in Parrish
Great surfing in SRQ happening soon!

Big birthday bar b q Sunday has been renamed Hurricane/Birthday party.
The show must go on.
Sounds like Ocla could be in for a ruff ride. Hope not, this my first summer in FL feeling very nervous.

Tropical storms are not that intense, especially as far inland as Ocala. As soon as the storm hits land it looses strength quickly. Lifelong Floridian here Be prepared for a long thunderstorm and maybe some wind gusts. Try to not panic and make sure you’re always prepared:) Denis is an awesome guy to follow!

eh, I disagree. Been in Ocala all my life and seen some intense tropical storms. Irma was a tropical storm when it hit us and did A LOT of damage. Lots of trees down, cars and roofs damaged and power outages for extended amount of time. So best to be prepared anyways . You never know .

Oh yeah Irma was definitely rough but I mean if the storm hits as a tropical storm, it will probably downgrade even more by the time it gets inland, don’t you think?
But either way, I’d say that you have better advice than I do on Ocala because you’re a local and I’m a coastal resident. Stay safe! 🙂
So, let's say I have a birthday party at noon on Sunday in East Pasco. Should it all be okay?
Thank you for your frequent updates!
We arrive in Pensacola Wednesday night. Will this effect us Wednesday through Sunday?
I’m pretty sure this is happening this Sunday (as in 2 days). It will be long gone by Wednesday.
Driving from Orlando to Saint Pete, Saturday morning and staying until Sunday morning. Would it be bad in the morning?
Are you expecting a slight shift west or east at this point from this track?
Denis Phillips is there a chance that shelters will open? USF is having graduation at Yuengling Center tomorrow night?

Thoughts on driving from Houston to Destin
Tomorrow morning?
Us here in cape coral/ Ft. Myers are pay attention to the rain as we have had a ton in the last month already.
like in June I believe it was
probably will have less then we did like those 3 days when we got a few feet

Eh cat 1 or tropical storm is really just a very big thunder storm. Bring light weight items in from the patio plan inside activities
And only check tv twice a day...or the news will have you drowning or worse by noon!
If it gets bigger than cat 1, freeze water in the you Tupperware ot zip lock bags. And be sure to have fuel for your grill.
And your good. Nothing to worry about. Just prepare.

Took them long enough to develop this one and show where it was going. Been watching it for over a week now, and for 8 days there was absolutely nothing there on satellite no rain no clouds nothing. Then the other day it appeared out of thin air right where they said it was at all this time. But it never once showed up on satellite. Weird really weird guess it was hiding in the Bermuda triangle or something.
We’re flying in to SRQ Saturday at 11:50 pm. Will our flight be cancelled? 😂
instigator…😉
Hey Denis, do you anticipate it strengthening at all when it gets into the gulf?
he said yesterday that it’s possible. No way to know for sure.

WHAT EFFECT IS IT GOING TO HAVE ON THE ST.PETE AREA?
tropical storm effects. The path goes right to Pinellas.
IT'S GOING TO BE REALLY RAINY AND MAYBE EVEN FREAKIN' WIMDY!!!!!!!!!
So, it’s going to rain Sunday?
Can it wait till Monday?
I know right?!

it's going to storm
meth will be wet
wind and rain

my plane arrives 8 PM Sunday. Flying allegiant arrive in Saint Pete Clearwater area.
Hey! Look at that! We're finally getting a storm in the daytime. It feels like all of the storms lately have been overnight.
Just go ahead and bank the center is coming to Polk county. We don’t get missed now 😂

I remember the last time we had a Tropical storm named Debbie that move slower than a snail. Was stuck with my client at his House for days because of severe Flooding in his neighborhood, well not this time spending it at my House!!!! Ps it also started on a Sunday morning 🌄
Thank you for the excellent updates. Moved to Suwannee County last year and I sure miss seeing you! Thanks again Dennis for keeping us updated.

beer
bidet!
toilet paper and water🙃🙃🙃
You mean alcohol?
The storm is moving at 16 miles an hour. That’s a good sign. Ian was moving slower than this storm.
A Southwest employee told me 50 people cancelled their flight we are getting on today to Orlando

U think we are ok to stay at home in Cortez fl
I'm grossly concerned of surge.
With this be hurricane force winds or tropical storm winds hitting the big bend area? Denis Phillips

Yes. LOL! I'm sorry, but couldn't resist. From what I have been reading, if it stays off the coast, it could strengthen to a hurricane. The present track doesn't seem to support that. I think we will know much more by tomorrow. All the best to you!
Time to Netflix and Chill!
got rid of Netflix!
a lot of people did. How about fire stick and chill! 😂
yea I still have Hulu. I am planning on working on my plant propagation station project. Stay safe and relaxed.
Better go stock up on TP!
Denis Phillips are you concerned about the chance of rapid intensification due to the warm waters once the storm gets over water again?
borderline hot water, 90 degree water Temps! Unless tons of wind sheer or Saharan dust this thing has the potential of rapid intensification!
no this is coming in at a bad angle, plus the mountains of Cuba won't allow for intensification. Also there is Saharian Dust around.
Lets hope our Grouper and Snapper are not chased away like Ian did.
keep your fingers crossed this doesn't impact our Sunday plans lol
I know I’ve been watching 🤞🏼🤞🏼
every storm track shows this and it always hits us in port charlotte
exactly
Getting a warm up drill!

In 2001 I met a friend who was visiting down in Florida with my kids at the beach. It was raining pretty hard at first and stopped. The winds were over 40 miles an hour. The whole beach was covered with suds even to the parking lot, I kept thinking there has to be some kind of tropical storm or depression, and never heard anything the whole day till the evening. It was never even forecasted, that was an adventure
that sounds like TS Gabrielle. Popped up out of nowhere on 9/11. My parents were out of power for a week. I had just flown back to Indiana 2 days prior.

it might be. It was quite an adventure and I’m so glad I didn’t know because I would’ve never gone to the beach with my kids and my friend and her husband wouldn’t have either. It’s one of those lifetime experiences..
If 30 mph winds hit the coast how bad will it be 50 miles inland? I live in an RV.
I think our daily summer thunderstorms are worse than that scenario would be. Just be prepared. Good luck where you are.
I think so too, but I'm keeping a close eye.
Of course. Stay safe.
Eek I’m driving to Tampa from Indianapolis leaving in the morning. Looks like it’s going to be a fun drive Sunday morning.
have plenty of gas and good wiper blades. Be safe.
please be careful ❤️
Rain-X the heck out of your windshield.

unless the waffle
houses close i’m not too worried

go get all the bread and toilet paper you can find!

u should be good for UFC Waffle House brunches
